VAL-CORE-006.evidence: Test `incremental_cursor_matches_full_rebuild` at scene.rs:2810. Builds two scenes: (1) full rebuild with cursor at position 13, (2) incremental update from cursor 0→13. Compares all cursor node fields (x, y, width, height, background) via assert_eq. Confirms the correctness invariant that incremental and full rebuild produce identical node properties for the same logical state.

VAL-CORE-007.evidence: No unit test specifically verifies that a scroll-only change (same text, different scroll offset) updates only N_DOC_TEXT and N_CURSOR while leaving chrome nodes unchanged. The test labeled VAL-CORE-007 (`data_buffer_exhaustion_triggers_full_rebuild` at scene.rs:2993) tests the data buffer exhaustion fallback path, not the normal scroll update path. Indirect evidence exists: (a) `scroll_runs_filters_above_viewport` tests the helper that filters non-visible lines, (b) `incremental_doc_update_changes_doc_text_and_cursor` (VAL-CORE-003) proves update_document_content only marks N_DOC_TEXT+N_CURSOR as changed while chrome nodes are byte-identical. However, the required direct evidence — a test with same text, different scroll_y, verifying only doc text runs and cursor change — is missing.
VAL-CORE-007.issues: Missing dedicated scroll-only incremental update test. The test labeled VAL-CORE-007 covers data buffer exhaustion, not scroll behavior. The update_document_content method in scene_state.rs does handle scroll correctly (calls scroll_runs to filter visible lines), but there is no test specifically demonstrating this with unchanged text and changed scroll offset.

VAL-CORE-008.evidence: Test `change_list_correct_per_update_type` at scene.rs:3098. Explicitly verifies change list contents for three update paths: (1) Clock update → change_list == [N_CLOCK_TEXT], (2) Cursor update → change_list == [N_CURSOR], (3) Document update → change_list contains N_DOC_TEXT and N_CURSOR (len=2). Note: selection update path is not explicitly verified in this test, but selection node marking is exercised in `incremental_selection_manages_nodes` and `selection_cycle_no_node_leak` which call mark_changed on cursor and selection node IDs.

VAL-CORE-009.evidence: Test `repeated_incremental_cycles_preserve_integrity` at scene.rs:3055. Runs 100 incremental cursor updates cycling through all byte positions in a 34-byte document. After 100 cycles, verifies: (a) cursor x/y matches expected position for the final state (computed via byte_to_line_col), (b) node count remains 8 (no drift), (c) static node properties preserved (root.width==1024, title_bar.height==36). Note: the test uses cursor-only updates rather than interleaved clock/cursor/text as the assertion ideally requests, but the drift-detection invariant (incremental result matches expected full-rebuild state) is verified.

VAL-CORE-010.evidence: Two tests cover this assertion: (1) `incremental_selection_manages_nodes` at scene.rs:2881 — adds one selection rect (node count → 9), clears selection (node count → 8), verifying node ID allocation starts at index 8. (2) `selection_cycle_no_node_leak` at scene.rs:2933 — cycles select/deselect 10 times, each cycle adding 2 selection rects (nodes 8,9 → count=10) then clearing (count=8). Asserts node IDs are always 8 and 9 via assert_eq, proving no slot leak across 10 cycles. Both tests use set_node_count(WELL_KNOWN_COUNT) to truncate selection nodes before re-allocation, matching the pattern in SceneState::update_selection.

frictions.description: The test labeled VAL-CORE-007 in scene.rs (data_buffer_exhaustion_triggers_full_rebuild) does not match the VAL-CORE-007 assertion description (scroll change updates only visible line runs). The label appears to be a mislabel or the test was intended to cover a different aspect.

VAL-CORE-009 test uses only cursor moves (100 iterations) rather than interleaved clock ticks, cursor moves, and text insertions as the assertion description specifies. The drift-detection invariant is still verified but with narrower operation diversity.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 4 passed (VAL-CORE-006, VAL-CORE-008, VAL-CORE-009, VAL-CORE-010), 1 failed (VAL-CORE-007: no dedicated scroll-only incremental update test — the labeled test covers data buffer exhaustion instead).
